channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Arnold, Robinson run for more than 100 yards as Oklahoma stuns No. 7 Alabama 24-3
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Xavier Robinson ran for career highs of 107 yards and two touchdowns, and Oklahoma stunned No. 7 Alabama 24-3 on Saturday night to become bowl eligible and deal a severe blow to the Crimson Tide's chances of receiving a College Football Playoff berth.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penguins' Crosby scores 600th NHL goal
item.description: Sidney Crosby became the 21st player in NHL history to score 600 goals when he reached the mark in the second period against the Utah Hockey Club on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dan Scifo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Silver Bowl champs! McClymonds too much for Oakland in section title showdown
item.description: 2024 Oakland Section championship: McClymonds wins 15th consecutive Silver Bowl as Berell Staples throws for two touchdowns
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: After fans storm field early, No. 21 Arizona St stops Hail Mary try for 28-23 win over No. 14 BYU
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Cam Skattebo was the workhorse once again for Arizona State, carrying the Sun Devils on his back by rushing for 147 yards and three touchdowns. His reward was a well-earned celebration with fans. Twice. Skattebo had another big game, Sam Leavitt threw for 247 yards and No.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Devin Neal scores 4 times, Kansas beats No. 16 Colorado 37-21 as Buffs' Big 12 title hopes take hit
item.description: Devin Neal ran for 207 yards and three touchdowns, caught four passes for 80 yards and another score, and led Kansas to a 37-21 victory over No. 16 Colorado on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Verstappen captures 4th F1 championship after Mercedes sweep of Las Vegas Grand Prix
item.description: Max Verstappen cruised to a fourth consecutive Formula 1 championship Saturday night by finishing fifth in the Las Vegas Grand Prix.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Andy Murray will coach Novak Djokovic through the Australian Open
item.description: The recently retired Andy Murray is going to team up with longtime rival Novak Djokovic as his coach, they both announced Saturday, with plans to prepare for — and work together through — the Australian Open in January.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Alyssa Nakken, first full-time female coach in MLB history, leaving Giants to join Guardians
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Alyssa Nakken, the first woman to coach in a Major League Baseball game, is leaving the San Francisco Giants to join the Cleveland Guardians. Nakken made history in 2022 when she took over as first-base coach following an ejection.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 76ers center Joel Embiid sidelined due to swelling in his left knee and will miss two games
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid is managing swelling in his left knee and will miss a second consecutive game Sunday, the team said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz blow 20-point lead to Spurs, lose fourth straight
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (AP) - Harrison Barnes had 25 points and 10 rebounds, rookie Stephon Castle scored 18 points and the San Antonio Spurs rallied to defeat the Utah Jazz 126-118 on Thursday night for their second straight win without Victor Wembanyama.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ohtani wins unanimous MVP after historic first season with Dodgers
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers' superstar Shohei Ohtani is the 2024 National League Most Valuable Player. It was a unanimous decision to award the Japanese phenom the 2024 MVP, and in doing so, create another historic moment as he becomes the first full-time designated hitter to win it.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Bountiful beats Roy to win 5A state title, 42-35
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Last month, Bountiful handed Roy its only regular season loss of the year, 42-35. It was a repeat performance in the championship game Thursday at Rice-Eccles Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mike Evans trending towards playing against the Giants on Sunday
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are looking to get back on track after four straight losses against the Giants, who swapped starting quarterbacks after losing five in a row.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alex Killorn helps the Ducks beat the Blackhawks 3-2 for their third straight win
item.description: Alex Killorn had a goal and an assist in the third period, and the Anaheim Ducks beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Tuesday night for their season-high third straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USU seeks to overturn loss in forfeited volleyball game against San Jose State
item.description: Just hours after Utah lawmakers urged Utah State University to join a Title IX lawsuit against the Mountain West Conference, the Logan-based school filed a motion to join the lawsuit, in part.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Ovechkin scores twice as Washington beats Utah, 6-2
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - One of the joys of having an NHL team is to watch the game's greatest players come to Salt Lake City. On Monday, the great Alex Ovechkin put on a show for the Utah Hockey Club fans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
